touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Mateus Hall at ArcForge introducing our conversation intelligence platform and its impact on rep productivity. Highlighted integration capabilities and automation features that reduce manual note-taking overhead.
next_steps: Mateus replied expressing interest in learning more about workflow automation features

touches.ai_summary: Initial qualification call with Mateus to understand ArcForge's current sales workflows and pain points. Discussed their team of 62 sellers struggling with post-call admin work and inconsistent CRM hygiene. Mateus mentioned they're exploring solutions including Fathom.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your team to look at conversation intelligence tools now?
questions_asked.answer: Our CRM data quality has declined as we've scaled, and reps are spending 30+ minutes per day on manual notes

questions_asked.question: How does your platform handle real-time coaching versus post-call analytics?
questions_asked.answer: We offer both live battle cards during calls and post-call analysis with auto-generated follow-ups

next_steps: Mateus to share more details about current tech stack
Schedule follow-up to dive deeper into automation needs

touches.ai_summary: Deep dive on ArcForge's tech stack and integration requirements. Mateus confirmed they use Salesforce and need seamless bi-directional sync. Discussed their 62-person sales team structure and how automations could save 10+ hours per rep weekly.

questions_asked.question: Beyond CRM hygiene, what other outcomes are you measuring success against?
questions_asked.answer: Manager visibility into deal health and faster onboarding for new reps are both critical

questions_asked.question: What's your implementation timeline typically look like for enterprise customers?
questions_asked.answer: Standard rollout is 2-3 weeks including training and custom workflow setup

next_steps: Rep to send integration documentation and case studies
Mateus to loop in RevOps to discuss data requirements

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sharing Salesforce integration guide and two case studies from similar-sized SaaS companies. Included ROI calculator showing potential 15-hour/week time savings across their team. Mateus responded asking about consulting services for workflow customization.

questions_asked.question: Do your consulting services help with change management and adoption strategy?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our Success team builds custom playbooks and runs manager training sessions

next_steps: Schedule call to scope consulting engagement

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call focused on consulting needs and customization requirements. Mateus detailed their current sales methodology and specific automations they want (auto-MEDDICC scoring, competitor mention alerts, deal risk flags). Discussed budget range and decision process.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in evaluating a tool like this?
questions_asked.answer: Our CEO Ava will need to sign off, and Lisa from RevOps needs to vet the data model

questions_asked.question: How configurable are the automation rules without engineering resources?
questions_asked.answer: Fully configurable through our workflow builder—no code required

next_steps: Mateus to introduce Lisa Koch from RevOps
Rep to prepare initial pricing estimate

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to address Mateus's questions about competitive differentiation versus Fathom. Walked through our XGBoost-based deal scoring and HNSW semantic search capabilities that Fathom doesn't offer. Mateus appreciated the technical depth and AI-native approach.

questions_asked.question: How does your AI handle industry-specific terminology in our vertical?
questions_asked.answer: Our models fine-tune on your historical calls within the first two weeks of deployment

next_steps: Send technical architecture whitepaper
Schedule discovery call with Lisa Koch

touches.ai_summary: Brief qualification check-in with Mateus to confirm next steps and timeline. He mentioned Fathom reached out with aggressive pricing but feels our platform is more aligned with their automation needs. Wants to move quickly to demo stage.

questions_asked.question: What's your ideal timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place within 6-8 weeks

next_steps: Confirm demo attendees and agenda

touches.ai_summary: Email to Lisa Koch and Mateus introducing discovery topics for RevOps review. Outlined data privacy controls, field mapping options, and reporting capabilities. Lisa responded with specific questions about custom object support and historical data migration.

questions_asked.question: Can we import and analyze historical call recordings from our previous tool?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we support bulk import for most major formats and will backfill insights

next_steps: Schedule technical discovery with Lisa

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sharing security documentation and SOC 2 compliance details that Lisa requested. Also included data retention policy and GDPR handling overview. Mateus confirmed they're ready to schedule demo with broader team.
next_steps: Calendar invite sent for product demo with Mateus, Lisa, and sales managers

touches.ai_summary: Quick call with Mateus to align on demo focus areas. He wants emphasis on manager dashboards and rep coaching workflows. Mentioned Valentina Clark (Sales Manager) will join and is skeptical about AI accuracy, so wants to see live examples.
next_steps: Customize demo with ArcForge's use cases
Prepare live call analysis example

touches.ai_summary: Pre-demo email to all attendees with agenda and demo environment access. Included short video showing the automation builder in action. Mateus confirmed all stakeholders are aligned and excited for the session.
next_steps: Demo scheduled for tomorrow

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Lisa Koch diving into Salesforce integration architecture, API rate limits, and custom field mapping. Lisa tested the workflow builder hands-on and validated it meets their RevOps requirements. Discussed data governance and role-based access controls.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle API call volume for a team our size with high call frequency?
questions_asked.answer: We use intelligent batching and caching—typical enterprise customers use <20% of rate limits

questions_asked.question: Can we set up different automation rules per sales segment?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, rules can be scoped by team, role, region, or custom attributes

questions_asked.question: What's your current call recording setup and storage?
questions_asked.answer: We use Zoom and store locally, but retention policies are inconsistent across teams

next_steps: Lisa to review technical documentation internally
Prepare integration requirements doc

touches.ai_summary: Shorter technical follow-up with Lisa to clarify webhook configuration and real-time sync behavior. She confirmed the technical setup meets requirements and is ready to recommend moving forward from a RevOps perspective.

questions_asked.question: If our Salesforce instance goes down, how does data queuing work?
questions_asked.answer: We queue up to 48 hours of updates and auto-retry with exponential backoff

next_steps: Lisa to provide formal technical signoff to Mateus

touches.ai_summary: Full product demo with Mateus, Lisa, and Valentina Clark. Walked through end-to-end workflow: call recording, AI summarization, automatic CRM updates, deal risk scoring, and manager dashboards. Valentina asked several pointed questions about AI accuracy and expressed concern about over-reliance on automation. Demonstrated confidence scores and human-in-the-loop options. Mateus was enthusiastic and Lisa confirmed technical fit.

questions_asked.question: What happens when the AI gets a summary wrong—how do reps correct it?
questions_asked.answer: Reps can edit any AI output inline, and those corrections improve the model over time

questions_asked.question: How does this compare feature-wise to Fathom?
questions_asked.answer: Fathom focuses on transcription and basic summaries; we add deal intelligence, predictive scoring, and deep CRM automation

questions_asked.question: Valentina, what would make you confident in rolling this out to your team?
questions_asked.answer: I'd need to see proof it doesn't hallucinate customer commitments or miss critical objections

objections: Valentina concerned about AI accuracy and potential for reps to become over-reliant on automation
next_steps: Offer pilot program to test accuracy with Valentina's team
Mateus to schedule exec alignment meeting with Ava

touches.ai_summary: Demo part two focused on manager coaching workflows and reporting. Showed how Valentina could review call snippets, track rep performance trends, and identify coaching opportunities. Also covered onboarding acceleration use case that Mateus highlighted earlier. Valentina warmed up after seeing granular controls and accuracy metrics.

questions_asked.question: Can I set up alerts for when reps miss key discovery questions?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, you can define custom triggers and get Slack or email notifications in real-time

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for getting this in front of Ava?
questions_asked.answer: Mateus: We have an exec review next week, I'll include this as a priority item

next_steps: Send proposal with pilot option for Valentina's team
Mateus to brief Ava before exec meeting

touches.ai_summary: Email to Mateus with formal proposal: $97,400 ACV for 62 seats including automations plus consulting package. Outlined 3-month pilot structure with Valentina's team to de-risk the rollout. Included ROI projection and implementation timeline. Mateus confirmed he's briefing Ava this week.
next_steps: Await feedback from Ava's briefing
Schedule exec meeting

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking in on Ava's availability and sharing executive summary deck tailored for CEO audience. Focused on business outcomes (revenue per rep, sales cycle reduction, forecast accuracy) rather than features. Mateus replied that Ava is interested and proposed two meeting slots.
next_steps: Exec meeting confirmed with Ava Desai

touches.ai_summary: Initial executive alignment meeting with Ava Desai and Mateus. Ava focused heavily on strategic fit and competitive positioning. She's familiar with Fathom and questioned why ArcForge should pay a premium for our platform. Explained our differentiation around predictive analytics and deep automation. Ava wants to see proof of ROI and customer references in similar growth stage.

questions_asked.question: What's your average customer ROI in the first year?
questions_asked.answer: Enterprise customers typically see 3-4x ROI through time savings and faster ramp time for new reps

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Fathom?
questions_asked.answer: We're positioned 30-40% higher but include consulting and advanced AI features they don't offer

objections: Ava concerned about price premium versus Fathom and wants stronger ROI justification
next_steps: Send customer references from similar-sized SaaS companies
Prepare detailed ROI analysis specific to ArcForge

touches.ai_summary: Email to Ava with three customer references (all SaaS companies, 50-100 reps) and detailed ROI breakdown showing $180k annual value from rep time savings alone. Also included case study demonstrating 22% sales cycle reduction. Ava acknowledged receipt and is reviewing with finance.
next_steps: Ava to review with CFO
Follow-up exec meeting to address remaining questions

touches.ai_summary: Brief email from Mateus indicating Ava is generally supportive but wants to negotiate pricing and discuss payment terms. He suggested scheduling a follow-up exec meeting to work through commercial details. Mentioned Fathom has been persistent with lower pricing offers.
next_steps: Schedule follow-up with Ava to discuss commercial terms

touches.ai_summary: Second executive meeting with Ava and Mateus to discuss commercial terms and address Fathom competitive pressure. Ava transparent about Fathom's pricing being significantly lower but acknowledged our platform is more comprehensive. Discussed multi-year options, payment terms, and pilot structure. Ava comfortable with strategic fit but needs to reconcile budget and wants concessions on pricing or services.

questions_asked.question: Would you consider a multi-year agreement with year-one discount?
questions_asked.answer: We can offer 15% discount on a 2-year commitment with annual pre-pay

questions_asked.question: Can we expand the consulting hours without increasing total ACV?
questions_asked.answer: We can add 10 additional consulting hours if you commit before month-end

questions_asked.question: What would make you comfortable moving forward in the next two weeks?
questions_asked.answer: Ava: I need to see a revised proposal with creative commercial terms and confirm our CFO approves

next_steps: Send revised proposal with multi-year discount option
Mateus to coordinate CFO review

touches.ai_summary: Email with revised proposal offering two options: (1) original $97,400 annual or (2) 2-year deal at $165k total ($82,500/year, 15% discount) with enhanced consulting. Highlighted month-end incentive with bonus implementation support. Mateus replied that CFO is reviewing and they're leaning toward multi-year option.
next_steps: Await CFO feedback
Prepare to address any final commercial questions

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking status of CFO review and offering to answer any financial or contract questions. Mateus confirmed they're close but legal is reviewing terms and security addendum. He expects resolution in next few days.
next_steps: Coordinate with legal on contract redlines

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled negotiation call with Mateus to finalize contract terms was rescheduled by ArcForge due to conflicting legal review meeting. Mateus apologized and proposed new time for two days later. No substantial discussion occurred.
next_steps: Rescheduled negotiation call

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Mateus to walk through legal redlines and security addendum. Most changes were standard liability caps and data processing terms. Mateus confirmed internal stakeholders are aligned and ready to move forward with 2-year option pending final exec signature from Ava.

questions_asked.question: Are there any remaining blockers on your side?
questions_asked.answer: No blockers—just need Ava's final signature and we're good to go

Route to Ava for signature
